posts.author: Daljit_Singh
posts.text: I have quite a different view about Edelweiss, I own it since COVID days and subsequently I put a lot of money into it and it’s around 40% of current portfolio value. So I can be not only biased but very biased. Your and mine perspectives can differ but that management has done a phenomenal job since ILFS crisis. Just have look at their peers DHFL RELIANCE HOME INDIABULLS. This is not mine but this is Parbai’s thesis that they created many good businesses over the year. It seemed very odd when you look at Alternatives Nuvama and ARC and they are in the top 2-3 businesses in India that’s litera…

posts.author: Chetanpinto
posts.text: My two cents on generally evaluating company management and leadership changes- Do I broadly trust the company management and their decision-making? If yes a leadership change shouldn’t matter. If on the other hand, I do not trust the company management and/or there has been some material change based on my own judgement (not only because of someone else taking some action), then I shouldn’t wait to exit. So it all boils down to whether the initial thesis of one’s investing still hold true or not, especially considering the current BFSI business environment… Pabrai or not. Investment horizons…

posts.author: Shidharth
posts.text: 23.25 time stamp onwards. This is the article verifying the information. Edelweiss Alternatives’ Subahoo Chordia quits, likely to launch own fund - The Economic Times Edelweiss Alternatives’ Subahoo Chordia quits, likely to launch own fund - The Economic Times

posts.author: Gaurav_Catalyst
posts.text: @Shidharth can you share the source or link for reference, if possible.

posts.author: Shidharth
posts.text: I had invested in this company primarily due to the fact that Mohnish Pabrai indicated that several businesses of the same mcap could be spun out of edelweiss at edelweiss’ current mcap. Which meant that I’m getting atleast 2-3 businesses for the price of one. However, while watching the latest investor presentation of Pabrai funds I came to know that the co-ceo of Edelweiss that Mohnish considered a better operator left. Just an Fyi.
